multiplechoiceresponse.label:
For the patient shown, the SHAP output indicates that:
cp_0 has a large positive SHAP value
thal_3 and oldpeak have negative SHAP values Which interpretation is most consistent with this local explanation?

choicegroup.choice: For this patient, the presence of cp_0 is one of the main reasons the model leans toward a higher-risk prediction, even though other features push in the opposite direction.
For this patient, cp_0 is more important than thal_3 and oldpeak in general, so it dominates the model’s reasoning overall.
Because thal_3 and oldpeak push risk down, the model would predict low risk if either one of them were absent.
Since cp_0 increases risk here, it would have the same effect for most patients with similar age and cholesterol.

div.p: A local SHAP explanation describes how individual features influenced the model’s prediction for a specific patient. A positive SHAP value means the feature pushes the prediction upward (toward higher risk). A negative SHAP value means the feature pushes the prediction downward (toward lower risk). In this case, cp_0 pushed the prediction toward higher risk, while other features pushed in the opposite direction. The explanation does not imply that one feature dominates all others in general, that removing a single feature would reverse the prediction, or that the same pattern necessarily applies to other patients. The key idea is that the model’s reasoning reflects a balance of influences for this particular individual.
